title: hedyotis pinifolia var. horridula
intro: Hedyotis pinifolia var. horridula, also known as Horrid Hedyotis or Rough-leaved Hedyotis, is a plant species within the family Rubiaceae, native to Australia and New Zealand. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Hedyotis pinifolia var. horridula are said to include wound healing, antiseptic properties, and potential antiviral effects, making it a valuable remedy for skin conditions and infections. Hedyotis pinifolia var. horridula is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be used in topical applications, such as creams and ointments, for its medicinal properties.
